**User**

same thing

---

**Cursor**

The display flashes something then goes blank, but firmware keeps running. Let me add debug to the flush callback to see if LVGL is actually drawing:

```c
#include "bsp_driver.h"
#include "driver/gpio.h"
#include "driver/i2c_master.h"
#include "driver/spi_master.h"
#include "driver/ledc.h"
#include "esp_lcd_panel_io.h"
#include "esp_lcd_panel_vendor.h"
#include "esp_lcd_panel_ops.h"
#include "esp_log.h"
#include "lvgl.h"
#include <time.h>
#include <string.h>

// --- LOCAL DRIVER INCLUDES ---
#include "esp_lcd_axs15231b.h"

static const char *TAG = "BSP_FACTORY";

// --- PINOUT (MATCHING FACTORY EXACTLY) ---
#define LCD_HOST    SPI2_HOST
#define PIN_CS      12
#define PIN_SCLK    5
#define PIN_D0      1
#define PIN_D1      2
#define PIN_D2      3
#define PIN_D3      4
#define PIN_BL      6
#define I2C_SDA     8
#define I2C_SCL     9
#define PMU_ADDR    0x34
#define EXP_ADDR    0x20

// I2C Device Addresses
#define QMI8658_ADDR    0x6B
#define AXP2101_ADDR    0x34
#define GT911_ADDR      0x5D
#define PCF85063_ADDR   0x51

static i2c_master_bus_handle_t i2c_bus = NULL;
static esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel_handle = NULL;

// Display flush callback (must be a function, not lambda - this is C, not C++)
static uint32_t flush_count = 0;
static void disp_flush_cb(lv_disp_drv_t *drv, const lv_area_t *area, lv_color_t *color_p) {
    flush_count++;
    // Log first few flushes and then periodically
    if (flush_count <= 5 || flush_count % 100 == 0) {
        ESP_LOGI("FLUSH", "#%lu: area(%d,%d)-(%d,%d)", 
                 (unsigned long)flush_count, area->x1, area->y1, area->x2, area->y2);
    }
    
    // !!! BYTE SWAP FIX !!!
    uint32_t len = (area->x2 - area->x1 + 1) * (area->y2 - area->y1 + 1);
    uint16_t *d = (uint16_t*)color_p;
    for(uint32_t i=0; i<len; i++) d[i] = (d[i] >> 8) | (d[i] << 8);
    esp_lcd_panel_draw_bitmap(panel_handle, area->x1, area->y1, area->x2 + 1, area->y2 + 1, color_p);
    lv_disp_flush_ready(drv);
}

// Device handles
static i2c_master_dev_handle_t qmi8658_handle = NULL;
static i2c_master_dev_handle_t axp2101_handle = NULL;
static i2c_master_dev_handle_t gt911_handle = NULL;
static i2c_master_dev_handle_t pcf85063_handle = NULL;

// --- STEP 1: I2C & PMU (THE FACTORY WAY) ---
esp_err_t bsp_i2c_init(void) {
    // 1. I2C Bus
    i2c_master_bus_config_t bus_conf = {
        .clk_source = I2C_CLK_SRC_DEFAULT,
        .i2c_port = -1,
        .scl_io_num = (gpio_num_t)I2C_SCL,
        .sda_io_num = (gpio_num_t)I2C_SDA,
        .glitch_ignore_cnt = 7,
        .flags = { .enable_internal_pullup = true }
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(i2c_new_master_bus(&bus_conf, &i2c_bus));

    // 2. PMU Init (The Secret Sauce)
    i2c_device_config_t pmu_cfg = { .dev_addr_length = I2C_ADDR_BIT_LEN_7, .device_address = PMU_ADDR, .scl_speed_hz = 100000 };
    i2c_master_dev_handle_t pmu;
    if (i2c_master_bus_add_device(i2c_bus, &pmu_cfg, &pmu) == ESP_OK) {
        // Enable LDOs
        uint8_t on[] = {0x90, 0xFF}; i2c_master_transmit(pmu, on, 2, -1);
        uint8_t on2[] = {0x91, 0xFF}; i2c_master_transmit(pmu, on2, 2, -1);
        // Set Voltages to 3.3V
        for(uint8_t r=0x92; r<=0x97; r++) {
            uint8_t v[] = {r, 0x1C}; 
            i2c_master_transmit(pmu, v, 2, -1);
        }
        axp2101_handle = pmu;
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "PMU Initialized (Factory Sequence)");
    }

    // 3. Expander Reset (The Other Secret Sauce)
    i2c_device_config_t exp_cfg = { .dev_addr_length = I2C_ADDR_BIT_LEN_7, .device_address = EXP_ADDR, .scl_speed_hz = 100000 };
    i2c_master_dev_handle_t exp;
    if (i2c_master_bus_add_device(i2c_bus, &exp_cfg, &exp) == ESP_OK) {
        uint8_t cfg[] = {0x03, 0x00}; i2c_master_transmit(exp, cfg, 2, -1);
        uint8_t low[] = {0x01, 0x00}; i2c_master_transmit(exp, low, 2, -1);
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(100));
        uint8_t high[] = {0x01, 0xFF}; i2c_master_transmit(exp, high, 2, -1);
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(200));
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Expander Reset Complete");
    }
    
    // 4. Initialize sensors
    bsp_qmi8658_init();
    bsp_pcf85063_init();
    bsp_touch_init();
    
    return ESP_OK;
}

// --- STEP 2: DISPLAY INIT (THE FACTORY WAY) ---
lv_disp_t *bsp_display_start(void) {
    // 1. SPI Bus
    spi_bus_config_t buscfg = {
        .data0_io_num = PIN_D0, .data1_io_num = PIN_D1,
        .data2_io_num = PIN_D2, .data3_io_num = PIN_D3,
        .sclk_io_num = PIN_SCLK,
        .max_transfer_sz = 320 * 480 * 2 + 100,
        .flags = SPICOMMON_BUSFLAG_MASTER | SPICOMMON_BUSFLAG_GPIO_PINS | SPICOMMON_BUSFLAG_QUAD,
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(spi_bus_initialize(LCD_HOST, &buscfg, SPI_DMA_CH_AUTO));

    // 2. Panel IO
    esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t io_handle = NULL;
    esp_lcd_panel_io_spi_config_t io_config = {
        .cs_gpio_num = PIN_CS, .dc_gpio_num = -1,
        .spi_mode = 0, .pclk_hz = 40 * 1000 * 1000,
        .trans_queue_depth = 10, .lcd_cmd_bits = 32, .lcd_param_bits = 8,
        .flags = { .quad_mode = 1 },
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_new_panel_io_spi((esp_lcd_spi_bus_handle_t)LCD_HOST, &io_config, &io_handle));

    // 3. Driver
    axs15231b_vendor_config_t vendor_config = { .flags = { .use_qspi_interface = 1 } };
    esp_lcd_panel_dev_config_t panel_config = {
        .reset_gpio_num = -1, .rgb_ele_order = LCD_RGB_ELEMENT_ORDER_RGB,
        .bits_per_pixel = 16, .vendor_config = (void*)&vendor_config,
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_new_panel_axs15231b(io_handle, &panel_config, &panel_handle));

    esp_lcd_panel_reset(panel_handle);
    esp_lcd_panel_init(panel_handle);
    esp_lcd_panel_invert_color(panel_handle, true);
    esp_lcd_panel_disp_on_off(panel_handle, true);

    // 4. LVGL
    lv_init();
    static lv_disp_draw_buf_t draw_buf;
    size_t buf_len = 320 * 40;
    lv_color_t *buf = heap_caps_malloc(buf_len * sizeof(lv_color_t), MALLOC_CAP_DMA);
    lv_disp_draw_buf_init(&draw_buf, buf, NULL, buf_len);

    static lv_disp_drv_t disp_drv;
    lv_disp_drv_init(&disp_drv);
    disp_drv.hor_res = 320; disp_drv.ver_res = 480;
    disp_drv.draw_buf = &draw_buf;
    disp_drv.flush_cb = disp_flush_cb;
    // Enable LVGL software rotation if needed
    disp_drv.rotated = LV_DISP_ROT_NONE;  // Try LV_DISP_ROT_90, LV_DISP_ROT_180, LV_DISP_ROT_270 if misaligned
    
    return lv_disp_drv_register(&disp_drv);
}

void bsp_display_backlight_on(void) {
    gpio_set_direction((gpio_num_t)PIN_BL, GPIO_MODE_OUTPUT);
    gpio_set_level((gpio_num_t)PIN_BL, 1);
}

void bsp_set_backlight(uint8_t brightness) {
    // PWM brightness control
    static bool ledc_initialized = false;
    if (!ledc_initialized) {
        ledc_timer_config_t ledc_timer = {
            .speed_mode = LEDC_LOW_SPEED_MODE,
            .duty_resolution = LEDC_TIMER_8_BIT,
            .timer_num = LEDC_TIMER_0,
            .freq_hz = 5000,
            .clk_cfg = LEDC_AUTO_CLK
        };
        ledc_timer_config(&ledc_timer);
        
        ledc_channel_config_t ledc_channel = {
            .gpio_num = PIN_BL,
            .speed_mode = LEDC_LOW_SPEED_MODE,
            .channel = LEDC_CHANNEL_0,
            .timer_sel = LEDC_TIMER_0,
            .duty = 0,
            .hpoint = 0
        };
        ledc_channel_config(&ledc_channel);
        ledc_initialized = true;
    }
    ledc_set_duty(LEDC_LOW_SPEED_MODE, LEDC_CHANNEL_0, (brightness * 255) / 100);
    ledc_update_duty(LEDC_LOW_SPEED_MODE, LEDC_CHANNEL_0);
}

void bsp_display_lock(uint32_t x) {}
void bsp_display_unlock(void) {}

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// QMI8658 ACCELEROMETER
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

esp_err_t bsp_qmi8658_init(void) {
    if (!i2c_bus) return ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
    
    i2c_device_config_t cfg = {
        .dev_addr_length = I2C_ADDR_BIT_LEN_7,
        .device_address = QMI8658_ADDR,
        .scl_speed_hz = 400000
    };
    
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_bus_add_device(i2c_bus, &cfg, &qmi8658_handle);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGW(TAG, "QMI8658 not found at 0x%02X", QMI8658_ADDR);
        return ret;
    }
    
    // Soft reset
    uint8_t reset[] = {0x60, 0xB0};
    i2c_master_transmit(qmi8658_handle, reset, 2, 100);
    vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(50));
    
    // Configure accelerometer: ±8g, 896Hz
    uint8_t ctrl2[] = {0x03, 0x05};  // CTRL2: Accel enable, ±8g
    i2c_master_transmit(qmi8658_handle, ctrl2, 2, 100);
    
    // Enable sensors
    uint8_t ctrl7[] = {0x08, 0x03};  // CTRL7: Enable accel + gyro
    i2c_master_transmit(qmi8658_handle, ctrl7, 2, 100);
    
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "QMI8658 Initialized");
    return ESP_OK;
}

esp_err_t bsp_qmi8658_read_accel(float *x, float *y, float *z) {
    if (!qmi8658_handle) {
        *x = *y = *z = 0;
        return ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
    }
    
    uint8_t reg = 0x35;  // Accel X low byte
    uint8_t data[6];
    
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_transmit_receive(qmi8658_handle, &reg, 1, data, 6, 100);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        *x = *y = *z = 0;
        return ret;
    }
    
    int16_t raw_x = (int16_t)((data[1] << 8) | data[0]);
    int16_t raw_y = (int16_t)((data[3] << 8) | data[2]);
    int16_t raw_z = (int16_t)((data[5] << 8) | data[4]);
    
    // Convert to g (±8g range)
    const float scale = 8.0f / 32768.0f;
    *x = raw_x * scale;
    *y = raw_y * scale;
    *z = raw_z * scale;
    
    return ESP_OK;
}

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// AXP2101 BATTERY
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

esp_err_t bsp_axp2101_read_battery(float *voltage, int *percent) {
    if (!axp2101_handle) {
        *voltage = 4.2f;
        *percent = 100;
        return ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
    }
    
    uint8_t reg = 0x78;  // Battery voltage register
    uint8_t data[2];
    
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_transmit_receive(axp2101_handle, &reg, 1, data, 2, 100);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        *voltage = 4.2f;
        *percent = 100;
        return ret;
    }
    
    uint16_t raw = ((data[0] & 0x3F) << 8) | data[1];
    *voltage = raw * 0.001f;  // mV to V
    
    // Estimate percentage (3.0V = 0%, 4.2V = 100%)
    float pct = (*voltage - 3.0f) / 1.2f * 100.0f;
    if (pct < 0) pct = 0;
    if (pct > 100) pct = 100;
    *percent = (int)pct;
    
    return ESP_OK;
}

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// GT911 TOUCH
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

esp_err_t bsp_touch_init(void) {
    if (!i2c_bus) return ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
    
    i2c_device_config_t cfg = {
        .dev_addr_length = I2C_ADDR_BIT_LEN_7,
        .device_address = GT911_ADDR,
        .scl_speed_hz = 400000
    };
    
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_bus_add_device(i2c_bus, &cfg, &gt911_handle);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGW(TAG, "GT911 not found at 0x%02X", GT911_ADDR);
        return ret;
    }
    
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "GT911 Touch Initialized");
    return ESP_OK;
}

esp_err_t bsp_touch_read(uint16_t *x, uint16_t *y) {
    if (!gt911_handle) return ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
    
    // Read status register (0x814E)
    uint8_t status_reg[] = {0x81, 0x4E};
    uint8_t status;
    
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_transmit_receive(gt911_handle, status_reg, 2, &status, 1, 100);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) return ret;
    
    if (!(status & 0x80)) {
        return ESP_ERR_NOT_FOUND;  // No touch
    }
    
    // Read point 1 (0x8150)
    uint8_t point_reg[] = {0x81, 0x50};
    uint8_t point[4];
    
    ret = i2c_master_transmit_receive(gt911_handle, point_reg, 2, point, 4, 100);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) return ret;
    
    *x = (point[1] << 8) | point[0];
    *y = (point[3] << 8) | point[2];
    
    // Clear status
    uint8_t clear[] = {0x81, 0x4E, 0x00};
    i2c_master_transmit(gt911_handle, clear, 3, 100);
    
    return ESP_OK;
}

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// PCF85063 RTC
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

esp_err_t bsp_pcf85063_init(void) {
    if (!i2c_bus) return ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
    
    i2c_device_config_t cfg = {
        .dev_addr_length = I2C_ADDR_BIT_LEN_7,
        .device_address = PCF85063_ADDR,
        .scl_speed_hz = 100000
    };
    
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_bus_add_device(i2c_bus, &cfg, &pcf85063_handle);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGW(TAG, "PCF85063 not found at 0x%02X", PCF85063_ADDR);
        return ret;
    }
    
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "PCF85063 RTC Initialized");
    return ESP_OK;
}

static uint8_t bcd2dec(uint8_t bcd) {
    return ((bcd >> 4) * 10) + (bcd & 0x0F);
}

esp_err_t bsp_pcf85063_read_time(struct tm *timeinfo) {
    if (!pcf85063_handle) {
        // Default time if RTC not available
        memset(timeinfo, 0, sizeof(struct tm));
        timeinfo->tm_hour = 12;
        return ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
    }
    
    uint8_t reg = 0x04;  // Seconds register
    uint8_t data[7];
    
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_transmit_receive(pcf85063_handle, &reg, 1, data, 7, 100);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        memset(timeinfo, 0, sizeof(struct tm));
        timeinfo->tm_hour = 12;
        return ret;
    }
    
    timeinfo->tm_sec = bcd2dec(data[0] & 0x7F);
    timeinfo->tm_min = bcd2dec(data[1] & 0x7F);
    timeinfo->tm_hour = bcd2dec(data[2] & 0x3F);
    timeinfo->tm_mday = bcd2dec(data[3] & 0x3F);
    timeinfo->tm_wday = data[4] & 0x07;
    timeinfo->tm_mon = bcd2dec(data[5] & 0x1F) - 1;
    timeinfo->tm_year = bcd2dec(data[6]) + 100;  // Years since 1900
    
    return ESP_OK;
}

```

Let me catch it from the very beginning:

The FLUSH messages show data is being sent to the display (5 flushes for the initial render), then nothing. But LVGL only redraws when content changes. The display shows content briefly then goes blank.

This could be the display entering sleep mode or power issues. Let me force continuous screen invalidation to keep it alive:

```cpp

/**
 * ╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
 * ║   PHENIX NAVIGATOR - Main Entry Point                                      ║
 * ║   Shield-Aligned UI with K4 Tetrahedron Visualization                      ║
 * ╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
 */

#include "esp_log.h"
#include "freertos/FreeRTOS.h"
#include "freertos/task.h"
#include "phenix_startup.h"
#include "lvgl.h"
#include "shield_ui.h"
#include "bsp_driver.h"

static const char *TAG = "PHENIX";

// Sensor polling timer
static lv_timer_t *sensor_timer = NULL;

/**
 * Sensor update callback - polls hardware and updates UI state
 */
static void sensor_update_cb(lv_timer_t *timer) {
    // Read accelerometer
    float ax, ay, az;
    if (bsp_qmi8658_read_accel(&ax, &ay, &az) == ESP_OK) {
        g_shield_state.accel_x = (int16_t)(ax * 1000);
        g_shield_state.accel_y = (int16_t)(ay * 1000);
        g_shield_state.accel_z = (int16_t)(az * 1000);
    }
    
    // Read battery
    float voltage;
    int percent;
    if (bsp_axp2101_read_battery(&voltage, &percent) == ESP_OK) {
        g_shield_state.battery = (uint8_t)percent;
    }
    
    // Read touch
    uint16_t tx, ty;
    if (bsp_touch_read(&tx, &ty) == ESP_OK) {
        g_shield_state.touch_active = true;
        g_shield_state.touch_x = tx;
        g_shield_state.touch_y = ty;
    } else {
        g_shield_state.touch_active = false;
    }
    
    // Read RTC time
    struct tm timeinfo;
    if (bsp_pcf85063_read_time(&timeinfo) == ESP_OK) {
        g_shield_state.hour = (uint8_t)timeinfo.tm_hour;
        g_shield_state.minute = (uint8_t)timeinfo.tm_min;
        g_shield_state.second = (uint8_t)timeinfo.tm_sec;
    }
    
    // Update UI with new state
    shield_ui_update();
}

/**
 * Serial output callback for Cognitive Shield bridge
 */
static void serial_telemetry_cb(lv_timer_t *timer) {
    // Output in Shield-parseable format
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "[ARCHITECT] F=%lu H=%luK S=0",
        (unsigned long)(esp_get_free_heap_size() / 1000),
        (unsigned long)(esp_get_minimum_free_heap_size() / 1000));
    
    printf("[ACCEL] X=%d Y=%d Z=%d\n",
        g_shield_state.accel_x,
        g_shield_state.accel_y,
        g_shield_state.accel_z);
    
    printf("[T] %s X=%d Y=%d\n",
        g_shield_state.touch_active ? "1" : "0",
        g_shield_state.touch_x,
        g_shield_state.touch_y);
    
    printf("[MODE] %d\n", (int)g_shield_state.mode);
    printf("[OK] V=%d S=%d B=%d\n",
        g_shield_state.voltage,
        g_shield_state.spoons,
        g_shield_state.battery);
}

extern "C" void app_main(void) {
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╔═══════════════════════════════════════╗");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "║   PHENIX NAVIGATOR INITIALIZING       ║");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╚═══════════════════════════════════════╝");
    
    // Initialize hardware via phenix_startup
    esp_err_t ret = phenix_startup_init();
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Startup failed! Error: %d", ret);
        while (1) {
            vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
        }
    }
    
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Hardware initialized. Starting Shield UI...");
    
    // Initialize Shield UI
    shield_ui_init();
    
    // DISABLED: Sensor polling and telemetry for debugging crash
    // Set up sensor polling (50ms interval)
    // sensor_timer = lv_timer_create(sensor_update_cb, 50, NULL);
    
    // Set up serial telemetry for Shield bridge (500ms interval)
    // lv_timer_create(serial_telemetry_cb, 500, NULL);
    
    // Callbacks disabled for debugging
    // shield_ui_on_voltage_change([](uint8_t v) {
    //     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "[VOLTAGE] Changed to %d", v);
    // });
    // 
    // shield_ui_on_spoons_change([](uint8_t s) {
    //     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "[SPOONS] Changed to %d", s);
    // });
    
    // Set display brightness
    phenix_set_brightness(80);
    
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╔═══════════════════════════════════════╗");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "║   PHENIX NAVIGATOR ONLINE             ║");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "║   Shield UI Ready                     ║");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╚═══════════════════════════════════════╝");
    
    // Main loop
    uint32_t loop_count = 0;
    while (1) {
        lv_timer_handler();
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(5));  // 5ms for smoother animations
        
        loop_count++;
        
        // Force screen redraw every 500ms to keep display alive
        if (loop_count % 100 == 0) {
            lv_obj_invalidate(lv_scr_act());
        }
        
        // Debug: log every 5 seconds to show we're alive
        if (loop_count % 1000 == 0) {
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "[HEARTBEAT] Loop %lu, Free heap: %lu bytes", 
                     (unsigned long)loop_count, 
                     (unsigned long)esp_get_free_heap_size());
        }
    }
}




```

**Now check the display - does the RED screen stay visible?** 

If it still goes blank, the issue is likely the I2C devices not responding (all those NACK errors). The PMU might not be keeping the display power rails on.

I understand the frustration. Let me check what's actually happening. The I2C errors are the problem - all I2C devices are failing. Let me create a minimal display-only test that bypasses I2C:

Let me try disabling the byte swap - it might be corrupting the display data:

```c
#include "bsp_driver.h"
#include "driver/gpio.h"
#include "driver/i2c_master.h"
#include "driver/spi_master.h"
#include "driver/ledc.h"
#include "esp_lcd_panel_io.h"
#include "esp_lcd_panel_vendor.h"
#include "esp_lcd_panel_ops.h"
#include "esp_log.h"
#include "lvgl.h"
#include <time.h>
#include <string.h>

// --- LOCAL DRIVER INCLUDES ---
#include "esp_lcd_axs15231b.h"

static const char *TAG = "BSP_FACTORY";

// --- PINOUT (MATCHING FACTORY EXACTLY) ---
#define LCD_HOST    SPI2_HOST
#define PIN_CS      12
#define PIN_SCLK    5
#define PIN_D0      1
#define PIN_D1      2
#define PIN_D2      3
#define PIN_D3      4
#define PIN_BL      6
#define I2C_SDA     8
#define I2C_SCL     9
#define PMU_ADDR    0x34
#define EXP_ADDR    0x20

// I2C Device Addresses
#define QMI8658_ADDR    0x6B
#define AXP2101_ADDR    0x34
#define GT911_ADDR      0x5D
#define PCF85063_ADDR   0x51

static i2c_master_bus_handle_t i2c_bus = NULL;
static esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel_handle = NULL;

// Display flush callback (must be a function, not lambda - this is C, not C++)
static uint32_t flush_count = 0;
static void disp_flush_cb(lv_disp_drv_t *drv, const lv_area_t *area, lv_color_t *color_p) {
    flush_count++;
    // Log first few flushes and then periodically
    if (flush_count <= 5 || flush_count % 100 == 0) {
        ESP_LOGI("FLUSH", "#%lu: area(%d,%d)-(%d,%d)", 
                 (unsigned long)flush_count, area->x1, area->y1, area->x2, area->y2);
    }
    
    // NO BYTE SWAP - try without it first
    // uint32_t len = (area->x2 - area->x1 + 1) * (area->y2 - area->y1 + 1);
    // uint16_t *d = (uint16_t*)color_p;
    // for(uint32_t i=0; i<len; i++) d[i] = (d[i] >> 8) | (d[i] << 8);
    
    esp_lcd_panel_draw_bitmap(panel_handle, area->x1, area->y1, area->x2 + 1, area->y2 + 1, color_p);
    lv_disp_flush_ready(drv);
}

// Device handles
static i2c_master_dev_handle_t qmi8658_handle = NULL;
static i2c_master_dev_handle_t axp2101_handle = NULL;
static i2c_master_dev_handle_t gt911_handle = NULL;
static i2c_master_dev_handle_t pcf85063_handle = NULL;

// --- STEP 1: I2C & PMU (THE FACTORY WAY) ---
esp_err_t bsp_i2c_init(void) {
    // 1. I2C Bus
    i2c_master_bus_config_t bus_conf = {
        .clk_source = I2C_CLK_SRC_DEFAULT,
        .i2c_port = -1,
        .scl_io_num = (gpio_num_t)I2C_SCL,
        .sda_io_num = (gpio_num_t)I2C_SDA,
        .glitch_ignore_cnt = 7,
        .flags = { .enable_internal_pullup = true }
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(i2c_new_master_bus(&bus_conf, &i2c_bus));

    // 2. PMU Init (The Secret Sauce)
    i2c_device_config_t pmu_cfg = { .dev_addr_length = I2C_ADDR_BIT_LEN_7, .device_address = PMU_ADDR, .scl_speed_hz = 100000 };
    i2c_master_dev_handle_t pmu;
    if (i2c_master_bus_add_device(i2c_bus, &pmu_cfg, &pmu) == ESP_OK) {
        // Enable LDOs
        uint8_t on[] = {0x90, 0xFF}; i2c_master_transmit(pmu, on, 2, -1);
        uint8_t on2[] = {0x91, 0xFF}; i2c_master_transmit(pmu, on2, 2, -1);
        // Set Voltages to 3.3V
        for(uint8_t r=0x92; r<=0x97; r++) {
            uint8_t v[] = {r, 0x1C}; 
            i2c_master_transmit(pmu, v, 2, -1);
        }
        axp2101_handle = pmu;
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "PMU Initialized (Factory Sequence)");
    }

    // 3. Expander Reset (The Other Secret Sauce)
    i2c_device_config_t exp_cfg = { .dev_addr_length = I2C_ADDR_BIT_LEN_7, .device_address = EXP_ADDR, .scl_speed_hz = 100000 };
    i2c_master_dev_handle_t exp;
    if (i2c_master_bus_add_device(i2c_bus, &exp_cfg, &exp) == ESP_OK) {
        uint8_t cfg[] = {0x03, 0x00}; i2c_master_transmit(exp, cfg, 2, -1);
        uint8_t low[] = {0x01, 0x00}; i2c_master_transmit(exp, low, 2, -1);
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(100));
        uint8_t high[] = {0x01, 0xFF}; i2c_master_transmit(exp, high, 2, -1);
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(200));
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Expander Reset Complete");
    }
    
    // 4. Initialize sensors
    bsp_qmi8658_init();
    bsp_pcf85063_init();
    bsp_touch_init();
    
    return ESP_OK;
}

// --- STEP 2: DISPLAY INIT (THE FACTORY WAY) ---
lv_disp_t *bsp_display_start(void) {
    // 1. SPI Bus
    spi_bus_config_t buscfg = {
        .data0_io_num = PIN_D0, .data1_io_num = PIN_D1,
        .data2_io_num = PIN_D2, .data3_io_num = PIN_D3,
        .sclk_io_num = PIN_SCLK,
        .max_transfer_sz = 320 * 480 * 2 + 100,
        .flags = SPICOMMON_BUSFLAG_MASTER | SPICOMMON_BUSFLAG_GPIO_PINS | SPICOMMON_BUSFLAG_QUAD,
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(spi_bus_initialize(LCD_HOST, &buscfg, SPI_DMA_CH_AUTO));

    // 2. Panel IO
    esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t io_handle = NULL;
    esp_lcd_panel_io_spi_config_t io_config = {
        .cs_gpio_num = PIN_CS, .dc_gpio_num = -1,
        .spi_mode = 0, .pclk_hz = 40 * 1000 * 1000,
        .trans_queue_depth = 10, .lcd_cmd_bits = 32, .lcd_param_bits = 8,
        .flags = { .quad_mode = 1 },
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_new_panel_io_spi((esp_lcd_spi_bus_handle_t)LCD_HOST, &io_config, &io_handle));

    // 3. Driver
    axs15231b_vendor_config_t vendor_config = { .flags = { .use_qspi_interface = 1 } };
    esp_lcd_panel_dev_config_t panel_config = {
        .reset_gpio_num = -1, .rgb_ele_order = LCD_RGB_ELEMENT_ORDER_RGB,
        .bits_per_pixel = 16, .vendor_config = (void*)&vendor_config,
    };
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_new_panel_axs15231b(io_handle, &panel_config, &panel_handle));

    esp_lcd_panel_reset(panel_handle);
    esp_lcd_panel_init(panel_handle);
    esp_lcd_panel_invert_color(panel_handle, true);
    esp_lcd_panel_disp_on_off(panel_handle, true);

    // 4. LVGL
    lv_init();
    static lv_disp_draw_buf_t draw_buf;
    size_t buf_len = 320 * 40;
    lv_color_t *buf = heap_caps_malloc(buf_len * sizeof(lv_color_t), MALLOC_CAP_DMA);
    lv_disp_draw_buf_init(&draw_buf, buf, NULL, buf_len);

    static lv_disp_drv_t disp_drv;
    lv_disp_drv_init(&disp_drv);
    disp_drv.hor_res = 320; disp_drv.ver_res = 480;
    disp_drv.draw_buf = &draw_buf;
    disp_drv.flush_cb = disp_flush_cb;
    // Enable LVGL software rotation if needed
    disp_drv.rotated = LV_DISP_ROT_NONE;  // Try LV_DISP_ROT_90, LV_DISP_ROT_180, LV_DISP_ROT_270 if misaligned
    
    return lv_disp_drv_register(&disp_drv);
}

void bsp_display_backlight_on(void) {
    gpio_set_direction((gpio_num_t)PIN_BL, GPIO_MODE_OUTPUT);
    gpio_set_level((gpio_num_t)PIN_BL, 1);
}

void bsp_set_backlight(uint8_t brightness) {
    // PWM brightness control
    static bool ledc_initialized = false;
    if (!ledc_initialized) {
        ledc_timer_config_t ledc_timer = {
            .speed_mode = LEDC_LOW_SPEED_MODE,
            .duty_resolution = LEDC_TIMER_8_BIT,
            .timer_num = LEDC_TIMER_0,
            .freq_hz = 5000,
            .clk_cfg = LEDC_AUTO_CLK
        };
        ledc_timer_config(&ledc_timer);
        
        ledc_channel_config_t ledc_channel = {
            .gpio_num = PIN_BL,
            .speed_mode = LEDC_LOW_SPEED_MODE,
            .channel = LEDC_CHANNEL_0,
            .timer_sel = LEDC_TIMER_0,
            .duty = 0,
            .hpoint = 0
        };
        ledc_channel_config(&ledc_channel);
        ledc_initialized = true;
    }
    ledc_set_duty(LEDC_LOW_SPEED_MODE, LEDC_CHANNEL_0, (brightness * 255) / 100);
    ledc_update_duty(LEDC_LOW_SPEED_MODE, LEDC_CHANNEL_0);
}

void bsp_display_lock(uint32_t x) {}
void bsp_display_unlock(void) {}

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// QMI8658 ACCELEROMETER
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

esp_err_t bsp_qmi8658_init(void) {
    if (!i2c_bus) return ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
    
    i2c_device_config_t cfg = {
        .dev_addr_length = I2C_ADDR_BIT_LEN_7,
        .device_address = QMI8658_ADDR,
        .scl_speed_hz = 400000
    };
    
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_bus_add_device(i2c_bus, &cfg, &qmi8658_handle);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGW(TAG, "QMI8658 not found at 0x%02X", QMI8658_ADDR);
        return ret;
    }
    
    // Soft reset
    uint8_t reset[] = {0x60, 0xB0};
    i2c_master_transmit(qmi8658_handle, reset, 2, 100);
    vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(50));
    
    // Configure accelerometer: ±8g, 896Hz
    uint8_t ctrl2[] = {0x03, 0x05};  // CTRL2: Accel enable, ±8g
    i2c_master_transmit(qmi8658_handle, ctrl2, 2, 100);
    
    // Enable sensors
    uint8_t ctrl7[] = {0x08, 0x03};  // CTRL7: Enable accel + gyro
    i2c_master_transmit(qmi8658_handle, ctrl7, 2, 100);
    
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "QMI8658 Initialized");
    return ESP_OK;
}

esp_err_t bsp_qmi8658_read_accel(float *x, float *y, float *z) {
    if (!qmi8658_handle) {
        *x = *y = *z = 0;
        return ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
    }
    
    uint8_t reg = 0x35;  // Accel X low byte
    uint8_t data[6];
    
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_transmit_receive(qmi8658_handle, &reg, 1, data, 6, 100);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        *x = *y = *z = 0;
        return ret;
    }
    
    int16_t raw_x = (int16_t)((data[1] << 8) | data[0]);
    int16_t raw_y = (int16_t)((data[3] << 8) | data[2]);
    int16_t raw_z = (int16_t)((data[5] << 8) | data[4]);
    
    // Convert to g (±8g range)
    const float scale = 8.0f / 32768.0f;
    *x = raw_x * scale;
    *y = raw_y * scale;
    *z = raw_z * scale;
    
    return ESP_OK;
}

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// AXP2101 BATTERY
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

esp_err_t bsp_axp2101_read_battery(float *voltage, int *percent) {
    if (!axp2101_handle) {
        *voltage = 4.2f;
        *percent = 100;
        return ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
    }
    
    uint8_t reg = 0x78;  // Battery voltage register
    uint8_t data[2];
    
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_transmit_receive(axp2101_handle, &reg, 1, data, 2, 100);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        *voltage = 4.2f;
        *percent = 100;
        return ret;
    }
    
    uint16_t raw = ((data[0] & 0x3F) << 8) | data[1];
    *voltage = raw * 0.001f;  // mV to V
    
    // Estimate percentage (3.0V = 0%, 4.2V = 100%)
    float pct = (*voltage - 3.0f) / 1.2f * 100.0f;
    if (pct < 0) pct = 0;
    if (pct > 100) pct = 100;
    *percent = (int)pct;
    
    return ESP_OK;
}

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// GT911 TOUCH
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

esp_err_t bsp_touch_init(void) {
    if (!i2c_bus) return ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
    
    i2c_device_config_t cfg = {
        .dev_addr_length = I2C_ADDR_BIT_LEN_7,
        .device_address = GT911_ADDR,
        .scl_speed_hz = 400000
    };
    
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_bus_add_device(i2c_bus, &cfg, &gt911_handle);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGW(TAG, "GT911 not found at 0x%02X", GT911_ADDR);
        return ret;
    }
    
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "GT911 Touch Initialized");
    return ESP_OK;
}

esp_err_t bsp_touch_read(uint16_t *x, uint16_t *y) {
    if (!gt911_handle) return ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
    
    // Read status register (0x814E)
    uint8_t status_reg[] = {0x81, 0x4E};
    uint8_t status;
    
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_transmit_receive(gt911_handle, status_reg, 2, &status, 1, 100);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) return ret;
    
    if (!(status & 0x80)) {
        return ESP_ERR_NOT_FOUND;  // No touch
    }
    
    // Read point 1 (0x8150)
    uint8_t point_reg[] = {0x81, 0x50};
    uint8_t point[4];
    
    ret = i2c_master_transmit_receive(gt911_handle, point_reg, 2, point, 4, 100);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) return ret;
    
    *x = (point[1] << 8) | point[0];
    *y = (point[3] << 8) | point[2];
    
    // Clear status
    uint8_t clear[] = {0x81, 0x4E, 0x00};
    i2c_master_transmit(gt911_handle, clear, 3, 100);
    
    return ESP_OK;
}

// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// PCF85063 RTC
// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

esp_err_t bsp_pcf85063_init(void) {
    if (!i2c_bus) return ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
    
    i2c_device_config_t cfg = {
        .dev_addr_length = I2C_ADDR_BIT_LEN_7,
        .device_address = PCF85063_ADDR,
        .scl_speed_hz = 100000
    };
    
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_bus_add_device(i2c_bus, &cfg, &pcf85063_handle);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGW(TAG, "PCF85063 not found at 0x%02X", PCF85063_ADDR);
        return ret;
    }
    
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "PCF85063 RTC Initialized");
    return ESP_OK;
}

static uint8_t bcd2dec(uint8_t bcd) {
    return ((bcd >> 4) * 10) + (bcd & 0x0F);
}

esp_err_t bsp_pcf85063_read_time(struct tm *timeinfo) {
    if (!pcf85063_handle) {
        // Default time if RTC not available
        memset(timeinfo, 0, sizeof(struct tm));
        timeinfo->tm_hour = 12;
        return ESP_ERR_INVALID_STATE;
    }
    
    uint8_t reg = 0x04;  // Seconds register
    uint8_t data[7];
    
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_transmit_receive(pcf85063_handle, &reg, 1, data, 7, 100);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        memset(timeinfo, 0, sizeof(struct tm));
        timeinfo->tm_hour = 12;
        return ret;
    }
    
    timeinfo->tm_sec = bcd2dec(data[0] & 0x7F);
    timeinfo->tm_min = bcd2dec(data[1] & 0x7F);
    timeinfo->tm_hour = bcd2dec(data[2] & 0x3F);
    timeinfo->tm_mday = bcd2dec(data[3] & 0x3F);
    timeinfo->tm_wday = data[4] & 0x07;
    timeinfo->tm_mon = bcd2dec(data[5] & 0x1F) - 1;
    timeinfo->tm_year = bcd2dec(data[6]) + 100;  // Years since 1900
    
    return ESP_OK;
}

```

Now let me update main.cpp to use the working init sequence:

```cpp
/**
 * PHENIX NAVIGATOR - Main Entry Point
 * Using WORKING display init from src.disabled
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include "nvs_flash.h"
#include "esp_log.h"
#include "freertos/FreeRTOS.h"
#include "freertos/task.h"

// Working BSP includes
#include "bsp_i2c.h"
#include "bsp_display.h"
#include "bsp_axp2101.h"
#include "lv_port.h"

#include "lvgl.h"
#include "esp_io_expander_tca9554.h"

static const char *TAG = "PHENIX";

#define EXAMPLE_LCD_H_RES 320
#define EXAMPLE_LCD_V_RES 480
#define LCD_BUFFER_SIZE (EXAMPLE_LCD_H_RES * EXAMPLE_LCD_V_RES)

// Global handles
esp_io_expander_handle_t expander_handle = NULL;
esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t io_handle = NULL;
esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel_handle = NULL;

void io_expander_init(i2c_master_bus_handle_t bus_handle)
{
    esp_err_t err = esp_io_expander_new_i2c_tca9554(bus_handle, ESP_IO_EXPANDER_I2C_TCA9554_ADDRESS_000, &expander_handle);
    if (err != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to create IO expander: %d", err);
        return;
    }
    err = esp_io_expander_set_dir(expander_handle, IO_EXPANDER_PIN_NUM_1, IO_EXPANDER_OUTPUT);
    if (err != ESP_OK) ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Set dir failed: %d", err);
    err = esp_io_expander_set_level(expander_handle, IO_EXPANDER_PIN_NUM_1, 0);
    if (err != ESP_OK) ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Set level low failed: %d", err);
    vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(100));
    err = esp_io_expander_set_level(expander_handle, IO_EXPANDER_PIN_NUM_1, 1);
    if (err != ESP_OK) ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Set level high failed: %d", err);
    vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(200));
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "IO Expander initialized");
}

extern "C" void app_main(void)
{
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╔═══════════════════════════════════════╗");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "║   PHENIX NAVIGATOR INITIALIZING       ║");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╚═══════════════════════════════════════╝");

    // NVS init
    esp_err_t ret = nvs_flash_init();
    if (ret == ESP_ERR_NVS_NO_FREE_PAGES || ret == ESP_ERR_NVS_NEW_VERSION_FOUND) {
        ESP_ERROR_CHECK(nvs_flash_erase());
        ret = nvs_flash_init();
    }

    // I2C bus init
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing I2C...");
    i2c_master_bus_handle_t i2c_bus_handle = bsp_i2c_init();

    // PMU init
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing PMU...");
    bsp_axp2101_init(i2c_bus_handle);

    // IO Expander init (for LCD reset)
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing IO Expander...");
    io_expander_init(i2c_bus_handle);

    // Display init
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing Display...");
    bsp_display_init(&io_handle, &panel_handle, LCD_BUFFER_SIZE);

    // Backlight
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing Backlight...");
    bsp_display_brightness_init();
    bsp_display_set_brightness(100);

    // LVGL port init
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing LVGL...");
    lv_port_init(io_handle, panel_handle, LCD_BUFFER_SIZE);

    // Start display keepalive task
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting display keepalive...");
    bsp_display_start_keepalive(panel_handle, io_handle, i2c_bus_handle, 5000);

    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╔═══════════════════════════════════════╗");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "║   PHENIX NAVIGATOR ONLINE             ║");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╚═══════════════════════════════════════╝");

    // Create simple test UI
    if (lvgl_port_lock(0)) {
        lv_obj_t *scr = lv_scr_act();
        lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(scr, lv_color_hex(0xFF0000), 0);  // RED background
        lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(scr, LV_OPA_COVER, 0);

        lv_obj_t *label = lv_label_create(scr);
        lv_label_set_text(label, "PHENIX NAVIGATOR");
        lv_obj_center(label);
        lv_obj_set_style_text_color(label, lv_color_hex(0xFFFFFF), 0);
        
        lvgl_port_unlock();
    }

    // Main loop
    uint32_t loop_count = 0;
    while (1) {
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(100));
        loop_count++;
        if (loop_count % 50 == 0) {
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "[HEARTBEAT] Loop %lu", (unsigned long)loop_count);
        }
    }
}

```

```cpp
/**
 * PHENIX NAVIGATOR - Main Entry Point
 * Using WORKING display init from src.disabled
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include "nvs_flash.h"
#include "esp_log.h"
#include "freertos/FreeRTOS.h"
#include "freertos/task.h"

// Working BSP includes
#include "bsp_i2c.h"
#include "bsp_display.h"
#include "bsp_axp2101.h"
#include "lv_port.h"

#include "lvgl.h"
#include "esp_io_expander_tca9554.h"

static const char *TAG = "PHENIX";

#define EXAMPLE_LCD_H_RES 320
#define EXAMPLE_LCD_V_RES 480
#define LCD_BUFFER_SIZE (EXAMPLE_LCD_H_RES * EXAMPLE_LCD_V_RES)

// Global handles
esp_io_expander_handle_t expander_handle = NULL;
esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t io_handle = NULL;
esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel_handle = NULL;
static lv_disp_t *lvgl_disp = NULL;

void io_expander_init(i2c_master_bus_handle_t bus_handle)
{
    esp_err_t err = esp_io_expander_new_i2c_tca9554(bus_handle, ESP_IO_EXPANDER_I2C_TCA9554_ADDRESS_000, &expander_handle);
    if (err != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to create IO expander: %d", err);
        return;
    }
    err = esp_io_expander_set_dir(expander_handle, IO_EXPANDER_PIN_NUM_1, IO_EXPANDER_OUTPUT);
    if (err != ESP_OK) ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Set dir failed: %d", err);
    err = esp_io_expander_set_level(expander_handle, IO_EXPANDER_PIN_NUM_1, 0);
    if (err != ESP_OK) ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Set level low failed: %d", err);
    vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(100));
    err = esp_io_expander_set_level(expander_handle, IO_EXPANDER_PIN_NUM_1, 1);
    if (err != ESP_OK) ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Set level high failed: %d", err);
    vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(200));
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "IO Expander initialized");
}

void lv_port_init_display(void)
{
    // Initialize LVGL port
    lvgl_port_cfg_t port_cfg = ESP_LVGL_PORT_INIT_CONFIG();
    port_cfg.task_priority = 4;
    port_cfg.task_stack = 1024 * 5;
    port_cfg.task_affinity = 1;
    port_cfg.task_max_sleep_ms = 500;
    port_cfg.timer_period_ms = 5;
    lvgl_port_init(&port_cfg);

    // Configure display
    lvgl_port_display_cfg_t disp_cfg = {};
    disp_cfg.io_handle = io_handle;
    disp_cfg.panel_handle = panel_handle;
    disp_cfg.buffer_size = LCD_BUFFER_SIZE;
    disp_cfg.sw_rotate = LV_DISP_ROT_NONE;
    disp_cfg.hres = EXAMPLE_LCD_H_RES;
    disp_cfg.vres = EXAMPLE_LCD_V_RES;
    disp_cfg.trans_size = LCD_BUFFER_SIZE / 10;
    disp_cfg.draw_wait_cb = NULL;
    disp_cfg.flags.buff_dma = false;
    disp_cfg.flags.buff_spiram = true;

    lvgl_disp = lvgl_port_add_disp(&disp_cfg);
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "LVGL display added: %p", (void*)lvgl_disp);
}

extern "C" void app_main(void)
{
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╔═══════════════════════════════════════╗");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "║   PHENIX NAVIGATOR INITIALIZING       ║");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╚═══════════════════════════════════════╝");

    // NVS init
    esp_err_t ret = nvs_flash_init();
    if (ret == ESP_ERR_NVS_NO_FREE_PAGES || ret == ESP_ERR_NVS_NEW_VERSION_FOUND) {
        ESP_ERROR_CHECK(nvs_flash_erase());
        ret = nvs_flash_init();
    }

    // I2C bus init
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing I2C...");
    i2c_master_bus_handle_t i2c_bus_handle = bsp_i2c_init();

    // PMU init
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing PMU...");
    bsp_axp2101_init(i2c_bus_handle);

    // IO Expander init (for LCD reset)
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing IO Expander...");
    io_expander_init(i2c_bus_handle);

    // Display init
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing Display...");
    bsp_display_init(&io_handle, &panel_handle, LCD_BUFFER_SIZE);

    // Backlight
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing Backlight...");
    bsp_display_brightness_init();
    bsp_display_set_brightness(100);

    // LVGL port init
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing LVGL...");
    lv_port_init_display();

    // Start display keepalive task
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting display keepalive...");
    bsp_display_start_keepalive(panel_handle, io_handle, i2c_bus_handle, 5000);

    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╔═══════════════════════════════════════╗");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "║   PHENIX NAVIGATOR ONLINE             ║");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╚═══════════════════════════════════════╝");

    // Create simple test UI
    if (lvgl_port_lock(0)) {
        lv_obj_t *scr = lv_scr_act();
        lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(scr, lv_color_hex(0xFF0000), 0);  // RED background
        lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(scr, LV_OPA_COVER, 0);

        lv_obj_t *label = lv_label_create(scr);
        lv_label_set_text(label, "PHENIX NAVIGATOR\nDisplay Working!");
        lv_obj_center(label);
        lv_obj_set_style_text_color(label, lv_color_hex(0xFFFFFF), 0);
        lv_obj_set_style_text_align(label, LV_TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER, 0);
        
        lvgl_port_unlock();
    }

    // Main loop - just heartbeat since lv_port handles LVGL task
    uint32_t loop_count = 0;
    while (1) {
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
        loop_count++;
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "[HEARTBEAT] %lu seconds", (unsigned long)loop_count);
    }
}
```

The bsp_axp2101.cpp uses Arduino library. Let me get the ESP-IDF version:

Let me create a simple PMU init without the Arduino library:

```c
/**
 * Simple AXP2101 PMU initialization using raw I2C
 * No XPowersLib dependency
 */

#include "bsp_axp2101.h"
#include "driver/i2c_master.h"
#include "esp_log.h"

static const char *TAG = "bsp_axp2101";

#define AXP2101_ADDR 0x34

static i2c_master_dev_handle_t pmu_handle = NULL;

static esp_err_t axp_write_reg(uint8_t reg, uint8_t val) {
    uint8_t data[2] = {reg, val};
    return i2c_master_transmit(pmu_handle, data, 2, -1);
}

void bsp_axp2101_init(i2c_master_bus_handle_t i2c_bus_handle) {
    if (i2c_bus_handle == NULL) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "I2C bus handle is NULL");
        return;
    }

    // Add PMU device to I2C bus
    i2c_device_config_t pmu_cfg = {
        .dev_addr_length = I2C_ADDR_BIT_LEN_7,
        .device_address = AXP2101_ADDR,
        .scl_speed_hz = 100000,
    };
    
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_bus_add_device(i2c_bus_handle, &pmu_cfg, &pmu_handle);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to add PMU device: %d", ret);
        return;
    }

    // Enable all LDOs
    axp_write_reg(0x90, 0xFF);  // LDOS enable 1
    axp_write_reg(0x91, 0xFF);  // LDOS enable 2
    
    // Set LDO voltages to 3.3V (0x1C = ~3.3V)
    for (uint8_t reg = 0x92; reg <= 0x97; reg++) {
        axp_write_reg(reg, 0x1C);
    }
    
    // Enable DC-DC converters
    axp_write_reg(0x80, 0xFF);

    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "AXP2101 PMU Initialized");
}

```

```cpp
#define __BSP_AXP2101_H__

#include "driver/i2c_master.h"

extern "C" {
#endif

void bsp_axp2101_init(i2c_master_bus_handle_t i2c_bus_handle);

}
```

The IO expander API expects old I2C driver. Let me use raw I2C instead:

```cpp
/**
 * PHENIX NAVIGATOR - Main Entry Point
 * Using WORKING display init from src.disabled
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include "nvs_flash.h"
#include "esp_log.h"
#include "freertos/FreeRTOS.h"
#include "freertos/task.h"

// Working BSP includes
#include "bsp_i2c.h"
#include "bsp_display.h"
#include "bsp_axp2101.h"
#include "lv_port.h"

#include "lvgl.h"

static const char *TAG = "PHENIX";

#define EXAMPLE_LCD_H_RES 320
#define EXAMPLE_LCD_V_RES 480
#define LCD_BUFFER_SIZE (EXAMPLE_LCD_H_RES * EXAMPLE_LCD_V_RES)

#define IO_EXP_ADDR 0x20

// Global handles
esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t io_handle = NULL;
esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel_handle = NULL;
static lv_disp_t *lvgl_disp = NULL;

void io_expander_reset(i2c_master_bus_handle_t bus_handle)
{
    // Add IO expander device to bus
    i2c_device_config_t exp_cfg = {
        .dev_addr_length = I2C_ADDR_BIT_LEN_7,
        .device_address = IO_EXP_ADDR,
        .scl_speed_hz = 100000,
    };
    i2c_master_dev_handle_t exp_handle;
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_bus_add_device(bus_handle, &exp_cfg, &exp_handle);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to add IO expander: %d", ret);
        return;
    }
    
    // Configure as output
    uint8_t cfg[] = {0x03, 0x00};  // Config register -> all outputs
    i2c_master_transmit(exp_handle, cfg, 2, -1);
    
    // Reset pulse: Low -> delay -> High
    uint8_t low[] = {0x01, 0x00};
    i2c_master_transmit(exp_handle, low, 2, -1);
    vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(100));
    
    uint8_t high[] = {0x01, 0xFF};
    i2c_master_transmit(exp_handle, high, 2, -1);
    vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(200));
    
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "IO Expander LCD reset complete");
}

void lv_port_init_display(void)
{
    // Initialize LVGL port
    lvgl_port_cfg_t port_cfg = ESP_LVGL_PORT_INIT_CONFIG();
    port_cfg.task_priority = 4;
    port_cfg.task_stack = 1024 * 5;
    port_cfg.task_affinity = 1;
    port_cfg.task_max_sleep_ms = 500;
    port_cfg.timer_period_ms = 5;
    lvgl_port_init(&port_cfg);

    // Configure display
    lvgl_port_display_cfg_t disp_cfg = {};
    disp_cfg.io_handle = io_handle;
    disp_cfg.panel_handle = panel_handle;
    disp_cfg.buffer_size = LCD_BUFFER_SIZE;
    disp_cfg.sw_rotate = LV_DISP_ROT_NONE;
    disp_cfg.hres = EXAMPLE_LCD_H_RES;
    disp_cfg.vres = EXAMPLE_LCD_V_RES;
    disp_cfg.trans_size = LCD_BUFFER_SIZE / 10;
    disp_cfg.draw_wait_cb = NULL;
    disp_cfg.flags.buff_dma = false;
    disp_cfg.flags.buff_spiram = true;

    lvgl_disp = lvgl_port_add_disp(&disp_cfg);
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "LVGL display added: %p", (void*)lvgl_disp);
}

extern "C" void app_main(void)
{
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╔═══════════════════════════════════════╗");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "║   PHENIX NAVIGATOR INITIALIZING       ║");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╚═══════════════════════════════════════╝");

    // NVS init
    esp_err_t ret = nvs_flash_init();
    if (ret == ESP_ERR_NVS_NO_FREE_PAGES || ret == ESP_ERR_NVS_NEW_VERSION_FOUND) {
        ESP_ERROR_CHECK(nvs_flash_erase());
        ret = nvs_flash_init();
    }

    // I2C bus init
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing I2C...");
    i2c_master_bus_handle_t i2c_bus_handle = bsp_i2c_init();

    // PMU init
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing PMU...");
    bsp_axp2101_init(i2c_bus_handle);

    // IO Expander reset for LCD
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Resetting LCD via IO Expander...");
    io_expander_reset(i2c_bus_handle);

    // Display init
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing Display...");
    bsp_display_init(&io_handle, &panel_handle, LCD_BUFFER_SIZE);

    // Backlight
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing Backlight...");
    bsp_display_brightness_init();
    bsp_display_set_brightness(100);

    // LVGL port init
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing LVGL...");
    lv_port_init_display();

    // Start display keepalive task
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting display keepalive...");
    bsp_display_start_keepalive(panel_handle, io_handle, i2c_bus_handle, 5000);

    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╔═══════════════════════════════════════╗");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "║   PHENIX NAVIGATOR ONLINE             ║");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╚═══════════════════════════════════════╝");

    // Create simple test UI
    if (lvgl_port_lock(0)) {
        lv_obj_t *scr = lv_scr_act();
        lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(scr, lv_color_hex(0xFF0000), 0);  // RED background
        lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(scr, LV_OPA_COVER, 0);

        lv_obj_t *label = lv_label_create(scr);
        lv_label_set_text(label, "PHENIX NAVIGATOR\nDisplay Working!");
        lv_obj_center(label);
        lv_obj_set_style_text_color(label, lv_color_hex(0xFFFFFF), 0);
        lv_obj_set_style_text_align(label, LV_TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER, 0);
        
        lvgl_port_unlock();
    }

    // Main loop - just heartbeat since lv_port handles LVGL task
    uint32_t loop_count = 0;
    while (1) {
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
        loop_count++;
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "[HEARTBEAT] %lu seconds", (unsigned long)loop_count);
    }
}
```

```c
#include "bsp_display.h"

#include "driver/gpio.h"
#include "driver/spi_master.h"
#include "driver/ledc.h"

#include "esp_lcd_axs15231b.h"


#include "esp_lcd_panel_io.h"
#include "esp_lcd_panel_vendor.h"
#include "esp_lcd_panel_ops.h"
#include "esp_lcd_panel_commands.h"
#include "esp_log.h"
#include <stdbool.h>
#include "bsp_i2c.h"
#include "driver/i2c_master.h"

static const char *TAG = "bsp_display";

static uint8_t g_brightness = 0;


static const axs15231b_lcd_init_cmd_t lcd_init_cmds[] = {
    {0xBB, (uint8_t[]){0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x5A, 0xA5}, 8, 0},
    {0xA0, (uint8_t[]){0xC0, 0x10, 0x00, 0x02, 0x00, 0x00, 0x04, 0x3F, 0x20, 0x05, 0x3F, 0x3F, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00}, 17, 0},
    {0xA2, (uint8_t[]){0x30, 0x3C, 0x24, 0x14, 0xD0, 0x20, 0xFF, 0xE0, 0x40, 0x19, 0x80, 0x80, 0x80, 0x20, 0xf9, 0x10, 0x02, 0xff, 0xff, 0xF0, 0x90, 0x01, 0x32, 0xA0, 0x91, 0xE0, 0x20, 0x7F, 0xFF, 0x00, 0x5A}, 31, 0},
    {0xD0, (uint8_t[]){0xE0, 0x40, 0x51, 0x24, 0x08, 0x05, 0x10, 0x01, 0x20, 0x15, 0x42, 0xC2, 0x22, 0x22, 0xAA, 0x03, 0x10, 0x12, 0x60, 0x14, 0x1E, 0x51, 0x15, 0x00, 0x8A, 0x20, 0x00, 0x03, 0x3A, 0x12}, 30, 0},
    {0xA3, (uint8_t[]){0xA0, 0x06, 0xAa, 0x00, 0x08, 0x02, 0x0A, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x00, 0x55, 0x55}, 22, 0},
    {0xC1, (uint8_t[]){0x31, 0x04, 0x02, 0x02, 0x71, 0x05, 0x24, 0x55, 0x02, 0x00, 0x41, 0x00, 0x53, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0x4F, 0x52, 0x00, 0x4F, 0x52, 0x00, 0x45, 0x3B, 0x0B, 0x02, 0x0d, 0x00, 0xFF, 0x40}, 30, 0},
    {0xC3, (uint8_t[]){0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x50, 0x03, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x01, 0x80, 0x01}, 11, 0},
    {0xC4, (uint8_t[]){0x00, 0x24, 0x33, 0x80, 0x00, 0xea, 0x64, 0x32, 0xC8, 0x64, 0xC8, 0x32, 0x90, 0x90, 0x11, 0x06, 0xDC, 0xFA, 0x00, 0x00, 0x80, 0xFE, 0x10, 0x10, 0x00, 0x0A, 0x0A, 0x44, 0x50}, 29, 0},
    {0xC5, (uint8_t[]){0x18, 0x00, 0x00, 0x03, 0xFE, 0x3A, 0x4A, 0x20, 0x30, 0x10, 0x88, 0xDE, 0x0D, 0x08, 0x0F, 0x0F, 0x01, 0x3A, 0x4A, 0x20, 0x10, 0x10, 0x00}, 23, 0},
    {0xC6, (uint8_t[]){0x05, 0x0A, 0x05, 0x0A, 0x00, 0xE0, 0x2E, 0x0B, 0x12, 0x22, 0x12, 0x22, 0x01, 0x03, 0x00, 0x3F, 0x6A, 0x18, 0xC8, 0x22}, 20, 0},
    {0xC7, (uint8_t[]){0x50, 0x32, 0x28, 0x00, 0xa2, 0x80, 0x8f, 0x00, 0x80, 0xff, 0x07, 0x11, 0x9c, 0x67, 0xff, 0x24, 0x0c, 0x0d, 0x0e, 0x0f}, 20, 0},
    {0xC9, (uint8_t[]){0x33, 0x44, 0x44, 0x01}, 4, 0},
    {0xCF, (uint8_t[]){0x2C, 0x1E, 0x88, 0x58, 0x13, 0x18, 0x56, 0x18, 0x1E, 0x68, 0x88, 0x00, 0x65, 0x09, 0x22, 0xC4, 0x0C, 0x77, 0x22, 0x44, 0xAA, 0x55, 0x08, 0x08, 0x12, 0xA0, 0x08}, 27, 0},
    {0xD5, (uint8_t[]){0x40, 0x8E, 0x8D, 0x01, 0x35, 0x04, 0x92, 0x74, 0x04, 0x92, 0x74, 0x04, 0x08, 0x6A, 0x04, 0x46, 0x03, 0x03, 0x03, 0x03, 0x82, 0x01, 0x03, 0x00, 0xE0, 0x51, 0xA1, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00}, 30, 0},
    {0xD6, (uint8_t[]){0x10, 0x32, 0x54, 0x76, 0x98, 0xBA, 0xDC, 0xFE, 0x93, 0x00, 0x01, 0x83, 0x07, 0x07, 0x00, 0x07, 0x07, 0x00, 0x03, 0x03, 0x03, 0x03, 0x03, 0x03, 0x00, 0x84, 0x00, 0x20, 0x01, 0x00}, 30, 0},
    {0xD7, (uint8_t[]){0x03, 0x01, 0x0b, 0x09, 0x0f, 0x0d, 0x1E, 0x1F, 0x18, 0x1d, 0x1f, 0x19, 0x40, 0x8E, 0x04, 0x00, 0x20, 0xA0, 0x1F}, 19, 0},
    {0xD8, (uint8_t[]){0x02, 0x00, 0x0a, 0x08, 0x0e, 0x0c, 0x1E, 0x1F, 0x18, 0x1d, 0x1f, 0x19}, 12, 0},
    {0xD9, (uint8_t[]){0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F}, 12, 0},
    {0xDD, (uint8_t[]){0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F, 0x1F}, 12, 0},
    {0xDF, (uint8_t[]){0x44, 0x73, 0x4B, 0x69, 0x00, 0x0A, 0x02, 0x90}, 8, 0},
    {0xE0, (uint8_t[]){0x3B, 0x28, 0x10, 0x16, 0x0c, 0x06, 0x11, 0x28, 0x5c, 0x21, 0x0D, 0x35, 0x13, 0x2C, 0x33, 0x28, 0x0D}, 17, 0},
    {0xE1, (uint8_t[]){0x37, 0x28, 0x10, 0x16, 0x0b, 0x06, 0x11, 0x28, 0x5C, 0x21, 0x0D, 0x35, 0x14, 0x2C, 0x33, 0x28, 0x0F}, 17, 0},
    {0xE2, (uint8_t[]){0x3B, 0x07, 0x12, 0x18, 0x0E, 0x0D, 0x17, 0x35, 0x44, 0x32, 0x0C, 0x14, 0x14, 0x36, 0x3A, 0x2F, 0x0D}, 17, 0},
    {0xE3, (uint8_t[]){0x37, 0x07, 0x12, 0x18, 0x0E, 0x0D, 0x17, 0x35, 0x44, 0x32, 0x0C, 0x14, 0x14, 0x36, 0x32, 0x2F, 0x0F}, 17, 0},
    {0xE4, (uint8_t[]){0x3B, 0x07, 0x12, 0x18, 0x0E, 0x0D, 0x17, 0x39, 0x44, 0x2E, 0x0C, 0x14, 0x14, 0x36, 0x3A, 0x2F, 0x0D}, 17, 0},
    {0xE5, (uint8_t[]){0x37, 0x07, 0x12, 0x18, 0x0E, 0x0D, 0x17, 0x39, 0x44, 0x2E, 0x0C, 0x14, 0x14, 0x36, 0x3A, 0x2F, 0x0F}, 17, 0},
    {0xA4, (uint8_t[]){0x85, 0x85, 0x95, 0x82, 0xAF, 0xAA, 0xAA, 0x80, 0x10, 0x30, 0x40, 0x40, 0x20, 0xFF, 0x60, 0x30}, 16, 0},
    {0xA4, (uint8_t[]){0x85, 0x85, 0x95, 0x85}, 4, 0},
    {0xBB, (uint8_t[]){0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00}, 8, 0},
    {0x13, (uint8_t[]){0x00}, 0, 0},
    {0x11, (uint8_t[]){0x00}, 0, 120},
    // 0x2C (Memory Write) removed - should not be in init sequence
};


void bsp_display_init(esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t *io_handle, esp_lcd_panel_handle_t *panel_handle, size_t max_transfer_sz)
{

    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Install panel IO");

    spi_bus_config_t buscfg = {};
    buscfg.sclk_io_num = EXAMPLE_PIN_LCD_SCLK;
    buscfg.data0_io_num = EXAMPLE_PIN_LCD_DATA0;
    buscfg.data1_io_num = EXAMPLE_PIN_LCD_DATA1;
    buscfg.data2_io_num = EXAMPLE_PIN_LCD_DATA2;
    buscfg.data3_io_num = EXAMPLE_PIN_LCD_DATA3;
    buscfg.max_transfer_sz = max_transfer_sz;

    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(spi_bus_initialize(EXAMPLE_SPI_HOST, &buscfg, SPI_DMA_CH_AUTO));


    // esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t io_handle = NULL;

    esp_lcd_panel_io_spi_config_t io_config = AXS15231B_PANEL_IO_QSPI_CONFIG(EXAMPLE_PIN_LCD_CS, NULL, NULL);
    io_config.pclk_hz = EXAMPLE_LCD_PIXEL_CLOCK_HZ;
    // Attach the LCD to the SPI bus
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_new_panel_io_spi((esp_lcd_spi_bus_handle_t)EXAMPLE_SPI_HOST, &io_config, io_handle));


    axs15231b_vendor_config_t vendor_config = {};
    vendor_config.init_cmds = lcd_init_cmds; // Uncomment these line if use custom initialization commands
    vendor_config.init_cmds_size = sizeof(lcd_init_cmds) / sizeof(lcd_init_cmds[0]);
    vendor_config.flags.use_qspi_interface = 1;

    esp_lcd_panel_dev_config_t panel_config = {
        .reset_gpio_num = EXAMPLE_PIN_LCD_RST,
        .rgb_ele_order = LCD_RGB_ELEMENT_ORDER_RGB,
        .bits_per_pixel = 16,
    };
    panel_config.vendor_config = (void *)&vendor_config;
    esp_lcd_new_panel_axs15231b(*io_handle, &panel_config, panel_handle);

    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_panel_reset(*panel_handle));
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_panel_init(*panel_handle));

    // Guard: Force the display ON and perform a quick white fill to override any "all pixels off" vendor command
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Forcing panel DISP ON and doing white fill test");
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_panel_disp_on_off(*panel_handle, true));
    vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(50));

    // quick white fill in 40-line chunks using plain uint16_t (portable)
    const int chunk_h = 40;
    const int chunk_w = 320; // panel width
    uint16_t *white_buf = (uint16_t *)heap_caps_malloc(chunk_w * chunk_h * sizeof(uint16_t), MALLOC_CAP_8BIT);
    if (white_buf) {
        for (int i = 0; i < chunk_w * chunk_h; ++i) white_buf[i] = 0xFFFF; // RGB565 white
        for (int y = 0; y < 480; y += chunk_h) {
            int yend = y + chunk_h;
            if (yend > 480) yend = 480;
            esp_lcd_panel_draw_bitmap(*panel_handle, 0, y, chunk_w, yend, (void*)white_buf);
        }
        free(white_buf);
    } else {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "white_buf allocation failed");
    }

    // ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_panel_invert_color(*panel_handle, true));
    // ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_panel_set_gap(*panel_handle, 0, 34));
    // ESP_ERROR_CHECK(esp_lcd_panel_mirror(*panel_handle, false, false));
    // Display will be turned on in main.cpp after full initialization
}

/* Keep-alive task: reassert SLPOUT + DISP_ON regularly and do a small white primer draw.
   Also periodically attempt to read MADCTL / COLMOD / PWRMODE to help diagnose state flips. */

static void display_keepalive_task(void *arg)
{
    struct ka_args_t { esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel; esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t io; i2c_master_bus_handle_t i2c_bus; uint32_t interval_ms; uint8_t last_madctl; uint8_t last_colmod; bool got_regs; } *args = (struct ka_args_t*)arg;
    if (!args) { vTaskDelete(NULL); return; }
    int counter = 0;

    // Attempt to read initial MADCTL/COLMOD so we can reassert them later
    if (args->io) {
        uint8_t v = 0;
        if (esp_lcd_panel_io_rx_param(args->io, 0x36, &v, 1) == ESP_OK) { args->last_madctl = v; args->got_regs = true; ESP_LOGI(TAG, "init MADCTL=0x%02X", v); }
        if (esp_lcd_panel_io_rx_param(args->io, 0x3A, &v, 1) == ESP_OK) { args->last_colmod = v; args->got_regs = true; ESP_LOGI(TAG, "init COLMOD=0x%02X", v); }
    }

    // If we couldn't read regs, set safe defaults (RGB565 / BGR)
    if (!args->got_regs) {
        args->last_colmod = 0x55; // RGB565
        args->last_madctl = LCD_CMD_BGR_BIT; // BGR bit
        args->got_regs = true; // mark as available so we will re-send these
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Keepalive: using default MADCTL=0x%02X COLMOD=0x%02X", args->last_madctl, args->last_colmod);
    }

    for (;;) {
        int tries = 0;
        bool awake = false;

        while (tries < 3 && !awake) {
            if (args->io) {
                // More conservative sequence: SLPOUT -> small delay -> NORON -> delay
                ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Keepalive: sending SLPOUT");
                esp_lcd_panel_io_tx_param(args->io, LCD_CMD_SLPOUT, NULL, 0);
                vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(350));
                ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Keepalive: sending NORON");
                esp_lcd_panel_io_tx_param(args->io, LCD_CMD_NORON, NULL, 0);
                vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(80));
            }

            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Keepalive: asserting DISP ON (API)");
            esp_lcd_panel_disp_on_off(args->panel, true);
            vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(120));

            // Re-send MADCTL/COLMOD if we have values
            if (args->io && args->got_regs) {
                ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Keepalive: re-sending MADCTL/COLMOD (0x%02X/0x%02X)", args->last_madctl, args->last_colmod);
                esp_lcd_panel_io_tx_param(args->io, LCD_CMD_MADCTL, &args->last_madctl, 1);
                esp_lcd_panel_io_tx_param(args->io, LCD_CMD_COLMOD, &args->last_colmod, 1);
            }

            // Small primer draw (40 lines) to ensure at least a small RAMWR happens
            const int primer_h = 40;
            const int primer_w = 320;
            size_t bytes = primer_w * primer_h * 2;
            uint16_t *prime = (uint16_t*)heap_caps_malloc(bytes, MALLOC_CAP_8BIT);
            if (prime) {
                for (int i = 0; i < primer_w * primer_h; ++i) prime[i] = 0xFFFF;
                esp_lcd_panel_draw_bitmap(args->panel, 0, 0, primer_w, primer_h, (void*)prime);
                free(prime);
            }

            // Check power mode to confirm we are awake
            if (args->io) {
                uint8_t pm = 0x00;
                esp_err_t r = esp_lcd_panel_io_rx_param(args->io, 0x0A, &pm, 1);
                if (r == ESP_OK) {
                    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "PWRMODE=0x%02X", pm);
                    // Heuristic: many panels return non-zero when awake
                    if (pm != 0x00) awake = true;
                } else {
                    ESP_LOGW(TAG, "PWRMODE read failed: %d", r);
                }
            } else {
                awake = true; // If no IO access, assume success
            }

            if (!awake) {
                ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Panel did not wake, retrying (%d)", tries+1);
                vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(220));
                tries++;
            }
        }

        if (!awake) {
            ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Keepalive failed to wake panel after retries; attempting panel reset + init");

            // Perform panel reset + init sequence and immediate white fill to recover
            esp_lcd_panel_reset(args->panel);
            vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(200));
            esp_lcd_panel_init(args->panel);
            vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(200));

            esp_lcd_panel_disp_on_off(args->panel, true);
            if (args->io) esp_lcd_panel_io_tx_param(args->io, LCD_CMD_DISPON, NULL, 0);
            vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(80));

            // quick full white fill
            const int chunk_h = 40;
            const int chunk_w = 320; // panel width
            uint16_t *white_buf = (uint16_t *)heap_caps_malloc(chunk_w * chunk_h * sizeof(uint16_t), MALLOC_CAP_8BIT);
            if (white_buf) {
                for (int i = 0; i < chunk_w * chunk_h; ++i) white_buf[i] = 0xFFFF; // RGB565 white
                for (int y = 0; y < 480; y += chunk_h) {
                    int yend = y + chunk_h;
                    if (yend > 480) yend = 480;
                    esp_lcd_panel_draw_bitmap(args->panel, 0, y, chunk_w, yend, (void*)white_buf);
                }
                free(white_buf);
            } else {
                ESP_LOGE(TAG, "white_buf allocation failed");
            }

            // If we have an I2C bus handle, attempt to re-enable PMU LDOs using PMU helper
            // PMU LDO toggle disabled - not implemented
            // if (args->i2c_bus) {
            //     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Keepalive: attempting PMU LDO toggle via PMU helper");
            //     extern esp_err_t bsp_pmu_toggle_ldos(void);
            //     esp_err_t r = bsp_pmu_toggle_ldos();
            //     if (r == ESP_OK) ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Keepalive: PMU LDO toggle succeeded"); else ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Keepalive: PMU LDO toggle failed: %d", r);
            // }
        }

        // Every 10 cycles: perform a full white fill to override any internal 'all pixels off' state
        if ((++counter % 10) == 0) {
            ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Keepalive: performing full white fill (cycle %d)", counter);
            const int chunk_h = 40;
            const int chunk_w = 320; // panel width
            uint16_t *white_buf = (uint16_t *)heap_caps_malloc(chunk_w * chunk_h * sizeof(uint16_t), MALLOC_CAP_8BIT);
            if (white_buf) {
                for (int i = 0; i < chunk_w * chunk_h; ++i) white_buf[i] = 0xFFFF; // RGB565 white
                for (int y = 0; y < 480; y += chunk_h) {
                    int yend = y + chunk_h;
                    if (yend > 480) yend = 480;
                    esp_lcd_panel_draw_bitmap(args->panel, 0, y, chunk_w, yend, (void*)white_buf);
                }
                free(white_buf);
            } else {
                ESP_LOGE(TAG, "white_buf allocation failed");
            }
        }

        // Periodic diagnostic reads (MADCTL, COLMOD, PWRMODE)
        if ((counter & 3) == 0 && args->io) {
            uint8_t val = 0;
            esp_err_t r;
            r = esp_lcd_panel_io_rx_param(args->io, 0x36, &val, 1); if (r == ESP_OK) ESP_LOGI(TAG, "MADCTL=0x%02X", val); else ESP_LOGW(TAG, "MADCTL read failed: %d", r);
            r = esp_lcd_panel_io_rx_param(args->io, 0x3A, &val, 1); if (r == ESP_OK) ESP_LOGI(TAG, "COLMOD=0x%02X", val); else ESP_LOGW(TAG, "COLMOD read failed: %d", r);
            r = esp_lcd_panel_io_rx_param(args->io, 0x0A, &val, 1); if (r == ESP_OK) ESP_LOGI(TAG, "PWRMODE=0x%02X", val); else ESP_LOGW(TAG, "PWRMODE read failed: %d", r);
        }

        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(args->interval_ms));
    }
}

void bsp_display_start_keepalive(esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel, esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t io, i2c_master_bus_handle_t i2c_bus, uint32_t interval_ms)
{
    if (interval_ms == 0 || panel == NULL) return;

    struct ka_args_t { esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel; esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t io; i2c_master_bus_handle_t i2c_bus; uint32_t interval_ms; uint8_t last_madctl; uint8_t last_colmod; bool got_regs; } *ka_args;
    ka_args = (struct ka_args_t*)heap_caps_malloc(sizeof(*ka_args), MALLOC_CAP_8BIT);
    if (!ka_args) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "keepalive alloc failed");
        return;
    }
    ka_args->panel = panel;
    ka_args->io = io;
    ka_args->i2c_bus = i2c_bus;
    ka_args->interval_ms = interval_ms;
    ka_args->last_madctl = 0;
    ka_args->last_colmod = 0;
    ka_args->got_regs = false;

    BaseType_t ok = xTaskCreate(display_keepalive_task, "display_ka", 4096, ka_args, tskIDLE_PRIORITY + 2, NULL);
    if (ok != pdPASS) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "failed to create keepalive task");
        free(ka_args);
    } else {
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "display keepalive started (interval %u ms)", (unsigned)interval_ms);
    }
}

void bsp_display_log_regs(esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel, esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t io)
{
    if (!io) { ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No IO handle for register reads"); return; }
    uint8_t val; esp_err_t r;
    r = esp_lcd_panel_io_rx_param(io, 0x36, &val, 1); if (r==ESP_OK) ESP_LOGI(TAG, "MADCTL=0x%02X", val); else ESP_LOGW(TAG, "MADCTL read failed: %d", r);
    r = esp_lcd_panel_io_rx_param(io, 0x3A, &val, 1); if (r==ESP_OK) ESP_LOGI(TAG, "COLMOD=0x%02X", val); else ESP_LOGW(TAG, "COLMOD read failed: %d", r);
    r = esp_lcd_panel_io_rx_param(io, 0x0A, &val, 1); if (r==ESP_OK) ESP_LOGI(TAG, "PWRMODE=0x%02X", val); else ESP_LOGW(TAG, "PWRMODE read failed: %d", r);
}


void bsp_display_brightness_init(void)
{
    // Prepare and then apply the LEDC PWM timer configuration
    ledc_timer_config_t ledc_timer = {};
    ledc_timer.speed_mode = LCD_BL_LEDC_MODE,
    ledc_timer.timer_num = LCD_BL_LEDC_TIMER;
    ledc_timer.duty_resolution = LCD_BL_LEDC_DUTY_RES;
    ledc_timer.freq_hz = LCD_BL_LEDC_FREQUENCY; // Set output frequency at 5 kHz
    ledc_timer.clk_cfg = LEDC_AUTO_CLK;
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(ledc_timer_config(&ledc_timer));

    // Prepare and then apply the LEDC PWM channel configuration
    ledc_channel_config_t ledc_channel = {};
    ledc_channel.speed_mode = LCD_BL_LEDC_MODE;
    ledc_channel.channel = LCD_BL_LEDC_CHANNEL;
    ledc_channel.timer_sel = LCD_BL_LEDC_TIMER;
    ledc_channel.intr_type = LEDC_INTR_DISABLE;
    ledc_channel.gpio_num = EXAMPLE_PIN_LCD_BL;
    ledc_channel.duty = 0, // Set duty to 0%
        ledc_channel.hpoint = 0;
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(ledc_channel_config(&ledc_channel));
}

void bsp_display_set_brightness(uint8_t brightness)
{
    if (brightness > 100)
    {
        brightness = 100;
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Brightness value out of range");
    }

    g_brightness = brightness;
    uint32_t duty = (brightness * (LCD_BL_LEDC_DUTY - 1)) / 100;

    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(ledc_set_duty(LCD_BL_LEDC_MODE, LCD_BL_LEDC_CHANNEL, duty));
    ESP_ERROR_CHECK(ledc_update_duty(LCD_BL_LEDC_MODE, LCD_BL_LEDC_CHANNEL));

    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "LCD brightness set to %d%%", brightness);
}

uint8_t bsp_display_get_brightness(void)
{
    return g_brightness;
}
```

Still blank. Let me check what the factory demo does differently - the lv_port might be the issue:

The factory defines `lv_port_init` in main.cpp directly. Let me do the same - remove the separate lv_port.c and inline it:

```cpp
/**
 * PHENIX NAVIGATOR - Minimal Working Display Test
 * Based directly on factory demo
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include "nvs_flash.h"
#include "esp_log.h"
#include "freertos/FreeRTOS.h"
#include "freertos/task.h"

#include "bsp_i2c.h"
#include "bsp_display.h"
#include "bsp_axp2101.h"
// #include "lv_port.h"  // Not using separate file

#include "lvgl.h"
#include "esp_lvgl_port.h"

static const char *TAG = "PHENIX";

#define EXAMPLE_LCD_H_RES 320
#define EXAMPLE_LCD_V_RES 480
#define LCD_BUFFER_SIZE (EXAMPLE_LCD_H_RES * EXAMPLE_LCD_V_RES)

#define IO_EXP_ADDR 0x20

// Global handles
esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t io_handle = NULL;
esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel_handle = NULL;
static lv_disp_t *lvgl_disp = NULL;

void io_expander_reset(i2c_master_bus_handle_t bus_handle)
{
    i2c_device_config_t exp_cfg = {
        .dev_addr_length = I2C_ADDR_BIT_LEN_7,
        .device_address = IO_EXP_ADDR,
        .scl_speed_hz = 100000,
    };
    i2c_master_dev_handle_t exp_handle;
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_bus_add_device(bus_handle, &exp_cfg, &exp_handle);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to add IO expander: %d", ret);
        return;
    }
    
    uint8_t cfg[] = {0x03, 0x00};
    i2c_master_transmit(exp_handle, cfg, 2, -1);
    
    uint8_t low[] = {0x01, 0x00};
    i2c_master_transmit(exp_handle, low, 2, -1);
    vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(100));
    
    uint8_t high[] = {0x01, 0xFF};
    i2c_master_transmit(exp_handle, high, 2, -1);
    vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(200));
    
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "IO Expander LCD reset complete");
}

// LVGL port init - inline like factory demo
void lv_port_init_display(void)
{
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing LVGL port...");
    
    lvgl_port_cfg_t port_cfg = ESP_LVGL_PORT_INIT_CONFIG();
    port_cfg.task_priority = 4;
    port_cfg.task_stack = 1024 * 5;
    port_cfg.task_affinity = 1;
    port_cfg.task_max_sleep_ms = 500;
    port_cfg.timer_period_ms = 5;
    
    esp_err_t ret = lvgl_port_init(&port_cfg);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "lvgl_port_init failed: %d", ret);
        return;
    }
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "LVGL port initialized");

    lvgl_port_display_cfg_t disp_cfg = {};
    disp_cfg.io_handle = io_handle;
    disp_cfg.panel_handle = panel_handle;
    disp_cfg.buffer_size = LCD_BUFFER_SIZE;
    disp_cfg.sw_rotate = LV_DISP_ROT_NONE;
    disp_cfg.hres = EXAMPLE_LCD_H_RES;
    disp_cfg.vres = EXAMPLE_LCD_V_RES;
    disp_cfg.trans_size = LCD_BUFFER_SIZE / 10;
    disp_cfg.draw_wait_cb = NULL;
    disp_cfg.flags.buff_dma = false;
    disp_cfg.flags.buff_spiram = true;

    lvgl_disp = lvgl_port_add_disp(&disp_cfg);
    if (lvgl_disp == NULL) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "lvgl_port_add_disp failed!");
        return;
    }
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "LVGL display added: %p", (void*)lvgl_disp);
}

extern "C" void app_main(void)
{
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╔═══════════════════════════════════════╗");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "║   PHENIX NAVIGATOR BOOT               ║");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╚═══════════════════════════════════════╝");

    // NVS
    esp_err_t ret = nvs_flash_init();
    if (ret == ESP_ERR_NVS_NO_FREE_PAGES || ret == ESP_ERR_NVS_NEW_VERSION_FOUND) {
        ESP_ERROR_CHECK(nvs_flash_erase());
        ret = nvs_flash_init();
    }

    // I2C
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Step 1: I2C init");
    i2c_master_bus_handle_t i2c_bus_handle = bsp_i2c_init();
    if (i2c_bus_handle == NULL) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "I2C FAILED!");
        return;
    }

    // PMU
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Step 2: PMU init");
    bsp_axp2101_init(i2c_bus_handle);

    // IO Expander reset
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Step 3: LCD reset via IO expander");
    io_expander_reset(i2c_bus_handle);

    // Display
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Step 4: Display init");
    bsp_display_init(&io_handle, &panel_handle, LCD_BUFFER_SIZE);
    if (panel_handle == NULL) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "DISPLAY FAILED!");
        return;
    }

    // Backlight
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Step 5: Backlight");
    bsp_display_brightness_init();
    bsp_display_set_brightness(100);

    // LVGL
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Step 6: LVGL");
    lv_port_init_display();

    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╔═══════════════════════════════════════╗");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "║   DRAWING TEST SCREEN                 ║");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╚═══════════════════════════════════════╝");

    // Create test UI
    if (lvgl_port_lock(0)) {
        lv_obj_t *scr = lv_scr_act();
        
        // RED background
        lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(scr, lv_color_hex(0xFF0000), 0);
        lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(scr, LV_OPA_COVER, 0);

        // White label
        lv_obj_t *label = lv_label_create(scr);
        lv_label_set_text(label, "PHENIX\nNAVIGATOR\n\nDisplay OK!");
        lv_obj_center(label);
        lv_obj_set_style_text_color(label, lv_color_hex(0xFFFFFF), 0);
        lv_obj_set_style_text_align(label, LV_TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER, 0);
        
        lvgl_port_unlock();
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "UI created - should see RED screen with white text");
    } else {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to lock LVGL!");
    }

    // Keepalive
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting display keepalive...");
    bsp_display_start_keepalive(panel_handle, io_handle, i2c_bus_handle, 5000);

    // Main loop
    uint32_t loop_count = 0;
    while (1) {
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
        loop_count++;
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "[HEARTBEAT] %lu sec, heap=%lu", (unsigned long)loop_count, (unsigned long)esp_get_free_heap_size());
    }
}

```

```cpp
/**
 * PHENIX NAVIGATOR - Minimal Working Display Test
 * Based directly on factory demo
 */

#include <stdio.h>
#include "nvs_flash.h"
#include "esp_log.h"
#include "freertos/FreeRTOS.h"
#include "freertos/task.h"

#include "bsp_i2c.h"
#include "bsp_display.h"
#include "bsp_axp2101.h"
#include "lv_port.h"

#include "lvgl.h"

static const char *TAG = "PHENIX";

#define EXAMPLE_LCD_H_RES 320
#define EXAMPLE_LCD_V_RES 480
#define LCD_BUFFER_SIZE (EXAMPLE_LCD_H_RES * EXAMPLE_LCD_V_RES)

#define IO_EXP_ADDR 0x20

// Global handles
esp_lcd_panel_io_handle_t io_handle = NULL;
esp_lcd_panel_handle_t panel_handle = NULL;
static lv_disp_t *lvgl_disp = NULL;

void io_expander_reset(i2c_master_bus_handle_t bus_handle)
{
    i2c_device_config_t exp_cfg = {
        .dev_addr_length = I2C_ADDR_BIT_LEN_7,
        .device_address = IO_EXP_ADDR,
        .scl_speed_hz = 100000,
    };
    i2c_master_dev_handle_t exp_handle;
    esp_err_t ret = i2c_master_bus_add_device(bus_handle, &exp_cfg, &exp_handle);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to add IO expander: %d", ret);
        return;
    }
    
    uint8_t cfg[] = {0x03, 0x00};
    i2c_master_transmit(exp_handle, cfg, 2, -1);
    
    uint8_t low[] = {0x01, 0x00};
    i2c_master_transmit(exp_handle, low, 2, -1);
    vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(100));
    
    uint8_t high[] = {0x01, 0xFF};
    i2c_master_transmit(exp_handle, high, 2, -1);
    vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(200));
    
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "IO Expander LCD reset complete");
}

// LVGL port init - inline like factory demo
void lv_port_init_display(void)
{
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Initializing LVGL port...");
    
    lvgl_port_cfg_t port_cfg = ESP_LVGL_PORT_INIT_CONFIG();
    port_cfg.task_priority = 4;
    port_cfg.task_stack = 1024 * 5;
    port_cfg.task_affinity = 1;
    port_cfg.task_max_sleep_ms = 500;
    port_cfg.timer_period_ms = 5;
    
    esp_err_t ret = lvgl_port_init(&port_cfg);
    if (ret != ESP_OK) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "lvgl_port_init failed: %d", ret);
        return;
    }
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "LVGL port initialized");

    lvgl_port_display_cfg_t disp_cfg = {};
    disp_cfg.io_handle = io_handle;
    disp_cfg.panel_handle = panel_handle;
    disp_cfg.buffer_size = LCD_BUFFER_SIZE;
    disp_cfg.sw_rotate = LV_DISP_ROT_NONE;
    disp_cfg.hres = EXAMPLE_LCD_H_RES;
    disp_cfg.vres = EXAMPLE_LCD_V_RES;
    disp_cfg.trans_size = LCD_BUFFER_SIZE / 10;
    disp_cfg.draw_wait_cb = NULL;
    disp_cfg.flags.buff_dma = false;
    disp_cfg.flags.buff_spiram = true;

    lvgl_disp = lvgl_port_add_disp(&disp_cfg);
    if (lvgl_disp == NULL) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "lvgl_port_add_disp failed!");
        return;
    }
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "LVGL display added: %p", (void*)lvgl_disp);
}

extern "C" void app_main(void)
{
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╔═══════════════════════════════════════╗");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "║   PHENIX NAVIGATOR BOOT               ║");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╚═══════════════════════════════════════╝");

    // NVS
    esp_err_t ret = nvs_flash_init();
    if (ret == ESP_ERR_NVS_NO_FREE_PAGES || ret == ESP_ERR_NVS_NEW_VERSION_FOUND) {
        ESP_ERROR_CHECK(nvs_flash_erase());
        ret = nvs_flash_init();
    }

    // I2C
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Step 1: I2C init");
    i2c_master_bus_handle_t i2c_bus_handle = bsp_i2c_init();
    if (i2c_bus_handle == NULL) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "I2C FAILED!");
        return;
    }

    // PMU
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Step 2: PMU init");
    bsp_axp2101_init(i2c_bus_handle);

    // IO Expander reset
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Step 3: LCD reset via IO expander");
    io_expander_reset(i2c_bus_handle);

    // Display
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Step 4: Display init");
    bsp_display_init(&io_handle, &panel_handle, LCD_BUFFER_SIZE);
    if (panel_handle == NULL) {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "DISPLAY FAILED!");
        return;
    }

    // Backlight
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Step 5: Backlight");
    bsp_display_brightness_init();
    bsp_display_set_brightness(100);

    // LVGL
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Step 6: LVGL");
    lv_port_init_display();

    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╔═══════════════════════════════════════╗");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "║   DRAWING TEST SCREEN                 ║");
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "╚═══════════════════════════════════════╝");

    // Create test UI
    if (lvgl_port_lock(0)) {
        lv_obj_t *scr = lv_scr_act();
        
        // RED background
        lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(scr, lv_color_hex(0xFF0000), 0);
        lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(scr, LV_OPA_COVER, 0);

        // White label
        lv_obj_t *label = lv_label_create(scr);
        lv_label_set_text(label, "PHENIX\nNAVIGATOR\n\nDisplay OK!");
        lv_obj_center(label);
        lv_obj_set_style_text_color(label, lv_color_hex(0xFFFFFF), 0);
        lv_obj_set_style_text_align(label, LV_TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER, 0);
        
        lvgl_port_unlock();
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "UI created - should see RED screen with white text");
    } else {
        ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to lock LVGL!");
    }

    // Keepalive
    ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Starting display keepalive...");
    bsp_display_start_keepalive(panel_handle, io_handle, i2c_bus_handle, 5000);

    // Main loop
    uint32_t loop_count = 0;
    while (1) {
        vTaskDelay(pdMS_TO_TICKS(1000));
        loop_count++;
        ESP_LOGI(TAG, "[HEARTBEAT] %lu sec, heap=%lu", (unsigned long)loop_count, (unsigned long)esp_get_free_heap_size());
    }
}
```
